name: Caesar's Secret
description: Decrypt this message: KHOOR ZRUOG. It uses a simple letter shift cipher.
hint: Try shifting each letter back by 3 positions in the alphabet.

name: Base64 Basics
hint: Base64 is an encoding scheme. Use an online decoder or Java's Base64 class.

name: Binary Message
hint: Each 8 bits = 1 ASCII character. Convert each byte separately.

name: Password Audit
description: A leaked password hash is: 5f4dcc3b5aa765d61d8327deb882cf99. What was the original password?
hint: This is an MD5 hash. Try searching it in an online hash lookup database.

name: XOR Simple
description: A single character was XORed with key 0x41 (decimal 65) to produce decimal value 47. What was the original character? Wrap it: FLAG{char}
hint: XOR is reversible. 47 XOR 65 = ? Then convert the result to its ASCII character.

name: Log Analyser
description:
From these log entries, which IP made the most requests?
192.168.1.1 x5
10.0.0.5 x12
172.16.0.1 x3
Wrap the answer: FLAG{IP_ADDRESS}
hint: Simply count the requests per IP. The attacker always sends the most.

name: ROT13 Reveal
hint: ROT13 shifts every letter by exactly 13 places. Apply it again to decode.

name: Hex Decode
description: Convert this hex string to readable text: 464c41477b4845585f4d4153544552007d
